Title: Substrate specificity and safener inducibility of the plant UDP-glucose-dependent family 1 glycosyltransferase super-family

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value detoxification enzyme for engineering broad xenobiotic conjugation capacity; suitable for stacking with GST/CYP detox genes, but caution that it also has endogenous monolignol-related roles.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to xenobiotic acceptors, catalysing O-glucosylation of phenols, N-glucosylation of anilines/herbicides, and some S-glucosylation of thiols.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes formation of xenobiotic glucosides; it was the dominant N-glucosyltransferase and one of the most active broad-spectrum UGTs in the screen.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant GST-fusion expression in E. coli; enzyme assays with 11 xenobiotic substrates; purified native-vs-fusion comparison; kinetic turnover comparison; metabolite/substrate profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fenclorim transcript induction analysis was performed at 100 µM for 4 h and 24 h, but UGT72B1 catalytic activity was defined mainly by in vitro enzyme assays.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in Arabidopsis; specific tissue enrichment not defined here. Arabidopsis tissues generally showed highest xenobiotic glucosylation in roots, cell cultures, foliage and flowers at extract level.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray: Arabidopsis fenclorim-response dataset referenced as GEO GSE28431; rice ArrayExpress E-MTAB-5524.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously reported to have endogenous roles in monolignol biosynthesis/cell wall lignification; structural work cited indicates noncanonical active-site arrangement explains unusual N-glucosylation capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential pleiotropic effects on endogenous phenylpropanoid/lignification metabolism if overexpressed.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Xenobiotic O-glucosides; N-glucosides of 3,4-dichloroaniline/metribuzin-related acceptors; minor S-glucosides
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 plant UDP-glycosyltransferase (UGT), group E; PSPG-box glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Major catalytic determinant for xenobiotic N-glucosylation in this study
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT72B1 showed exceptionally high and broad xenobiotic glycosylation activity. 2. It was almost uniquely responsible for strong N-glucosyltransferase activity toward 3,4-dichloroaniline. 3. It also catalysed O- and S-glucosylation, making it a multifunctional detoxification enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Arabidopsis tissues/cell extracts; specific organ accumulation not resolved
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Detoxification and stress protection through conjugation of foreign/toxic small molecules

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful broad-substrate detoxification gene for metabolic engineering; valuable where multi-substrate conjugation is desired, with caution for off-target conjugation of endogenous phenolics/flavonoids.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to hydroxyl, thiol and amino xenobiotic acceptors.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Broad-spectrum xenobiotic glucosyltransferase; the only enzyme reported active toward all 11 panel substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant expression and high-throughput enzyme assay across 11 xenobiotic substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro activity profiling; no specific environmental dependency established.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray: Arabidopsis GSE28431; rice E-MTAB-5524 (study-level datasets).
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unique ability in the panel to N-glucosylate both metribuzin and picloram; also previously shown to glucosylate quercetin at multiple hydroxyl positions.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad promiscuity may perturb endogenous small-molecule pools if deployed constitutively.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Multiple xenobiotic glucosides including O-, N- and S-glucosides
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: UDP-glucose plus all 11 tested xenobiotic acceptors
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting, but identified as an unusually broad-spectrum catalyst
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT88A1 glucosylated all 11 xenobiotic substrates tested. 2. It catalysed O-, S- and N-glucosylation. 3. It was uniquely able to N-glucosylate the herbicides metribuzin and picloram in this enzyme panel.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering phenolic xenobiotic detoxification, especially for O-glucosylation of chlorophenols and naphthol-like compounds.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to phenolic hydroxyl xenobiotic acceptors.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Highly active O-glucosyltransferase with appreciable activity toward 1-naphthol; no major N-glucosylation reported.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme expression and substrate activity screen.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Study-level microarray datasets: GSE28431 and E-MTAB-5524.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Together with UGT71C2, it was uniquely noted for appreciable activity toward 1-naphthol and acted essentially as an OGT.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential undesired conjugation of endogenous phenolics.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT71C1 was among the most active O-glucosyltransferases in the screen. 2. It preferentially glucosylated phenolic acceptors. 3. It showed notable activity toward 1-naphthol and little evidence for broad NGT function.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong O-glucosyltransferase candidate for engineering detoxification of phenolic contaminants.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to phenolic hydroxyl xenobiotic acceptors and shows limited activity on some other acceptor classes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: One of the highest-activity O-glucosyltransferases in the panel; strong activity toward multiple phenolic xenobiotics.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant expression and in vitro substrate screen.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Study-level microarray datasets: GSE28431 and E-MTAB-5524.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Like UGT71C1, it was highlighted for appreciable activity toward 1-naphthol; activity remained dominated by O-glucosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad acceptor usage may affect endogenous phenolic metabolism if overexpressed.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT71C2 showed very high activity toward phenolic xenobiotics. 2. It was one of the strongest OGTs identified. 3. It also showed appreciable activity toward 1-naphthol, distinguishing it within the screen.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate for engineering thiol-xenobiotic conjugation; may also affect endogenous sulfur-specialized metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to thiophenol sulfur acceptors more efficiently than to the model phenolic substrate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High S-glucosyltransferase activity toward all tested thiophenols, exceeding O-glucosylation activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay with thiophenol and phenolic xenobiotic substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Study-level microarray datasets: GSE28431 and E-MTAB-5524.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted as one of the most active SGTs; group L enzymes showed preference for sulfur acceptors relative to phenolic xenobiotics.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible interference with endogenous sulfur metabolite conjugation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT74F1 was one of the strongest thiol-conjugating UGTs. 2. Its SGT activity exceeded its OGT activity. 3. It contributes to the study’s evidence that some UGTs evolved selectivity for S-acceptors.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool for engineering sulfur-xenobiotic detoxification; caution because the enzyme has known endogenous glucosinolate-related function.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to thiol acceptors; also shows lower activity on other xenobiotic classes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High S-glucosyltransferase activity toward thiophenols, supporting a selective preference for S-acceptors.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay across xenobiotic substrate panel.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Study-level microarray datasets: GSE28431 and E-MTAB-5524.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes its endogenous role in glucosinolate biosynthesis via thiohydroximate conjugation, consistent with specialized S-acceptor evolution.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropy in glucosinolate metabolism if edited or overexpressed.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT74B1 displayed strong S-glucosyltransferase activity toward all tested thiophenols. 2. Its substrate bias supports functional specialization for sulfur acceptors. 3. This specificity is consistent with its known endogenous role in glucosinolate biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for sulfur-xenobiotic detoxification engineering with caution due to possible endogenous aromatic acid/auxin-related conjugation roles.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to thiol acceptors and shows lower activities on several phenolic/xenobiotic acceptors.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High S-glucosyltransferase activity, with thiophenol conjugation exceeding O-glucosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme screen over 11 xenobiotic substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Study-level microarray datasets: GSE28431 and E-MTAB-5524.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted among the strongest SGTs in group L.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could alter endogenous aromatic conjugate metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT84B1 was a high-activity thiol-conjugating UGT. 2. It favored S-acceptors over the phenolic benchmark substrate. 3. It supports the conclusion that some group L UGTs are specialized SGTs.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potentially useful for targeted triclosan/phenolic biocide detoxification.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to phenolic xenobiotic acceptors, with selective preference for triclosan noted in discussion.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shows selective preference for triclosan relative to many other UGTs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme screen across xenobiotic panel.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Study-level microarray datasets: GSE28431 and E-MTAB-5524.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper highlights UGT71D1 as the only enzyme showing selective preference toward triclosan.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could alter endogenous phenolic balance if constitutively expressed.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Triclosan O-glucoside and related phenolic glucosides
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT71D1 glucosylated phenolic xenobiotics. 2. It was singled out for selective preference toward triclosan. 3. This distinguishes it from the generally broader specificities of many other active UGTs.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: UDP-glucose-dependent xenobiotic glycosylation / detoxification stress response
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stress-inducible detoxification gene; candidate for stress-coupled detoxification engineering rather than constitutive high-flux detoxification.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to multiple xenobiotic acceptors with modest OGT and low SGT activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of the conserved Arabidopsis D-group stress/safener-inducible detoxification module and shows low but detectable xenobiotic conjugation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray induction after fenclorim treatment; Genevestigator co-induction analysis; recombinant enzyme screen.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fenclorim 100 µM, 4 h/24 h; also induced under multiple biotic and abiotic stress-related conditions in Genevestigator.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in Arabidopsis cell/root culture by fenclorim and by multiple stress-associated conditions/pathogens.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Arabidopsis microarray GEO GSE28431; Genevestigator meta-analysis; rice ArrayExpress E-MTAB-5524 at study level.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Knockout of UGT73B3 and UGT73B5 was cited to reduce resistance to Pseudomonas syringae, suggesting endogenous protective-metabolite detoxification roles beyond xenobiotics.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering defence-metabolite turnover may modify pathogen resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Low-level xenobiotic glucosides; likely endogenous stress-metabolite glucosides
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Phenolic and some sulfur-containing xenobiotics; exact preferred endogenous substrate unresolved here
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Core stress-response component, not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73B3 is fenclorim inducible and part of the Arabidopsis D-group stress-response signature. 2. It has modest xenobiotic glucosylation activity. 3. The paper links this class to protection against stress-generated toxic metabolites.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: UDP-glucose-dependent xenobiotic glycosylation / detoxification stress response
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising stress-responsive detoxification gene for inducible protection systems; may be better stacked with other D/L-group genes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to some xenobiotic acceptors, mainly as low OGT/SGT activity enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: One of the core Arabidopsis UGT-stress response genes consistently induced by fenclorim and many other perturbations.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Fenclorim microarray profiling; Genevestigator signature analysis; recombinant enzyme assays.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fenclorim 100 µM and multiple stress, pathogen, pesticide and oxylipin-related conditions in Genevestigator.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in all top 25 Arabidopsis conditions most similar to fenclorim treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted as a core component of the Arabidopsis UGT-stress response.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could modify endogenous defence-related metabolite pools.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Low-activity phenolic and related xenobiotic acceptors; endogenous substrate unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73B4 is strongly associated with the Arabidopsis safener/stress induction signature. 2. It has detectable but modest xenobiotic glucosylation activity. 3. It is proposed to contribute to protective conjugation during chemical and biotic stress.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: UDP-glucose-dependent xenobiotic glycosylation / detoxification stress response
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good candidate for inducible stress-protection engineering; must consider defence-related pleiotropy.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to selected xenobiotic acceptors with modest activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Core D-group safener/stress-inducible UGT associated with protective detoxification functions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Fenclorim-responsive microarray; Genevestigator co-induction; recombinant enzyme activity screen; literature knockout evidence cited in discussion.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fenclorim 100 µM, plus multiple chemical and pathogen-related perturbations.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated broadly across Arabidopsis stress-like treatments; fenclorim inducible.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion cites that knockout of UGT73B3 and UGT73B5 reduces resistance to Pseudomonas syringae, supporting endogenous defence-metabolite protection roles.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible altered pathogen resistance or defence signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Some phenolic/sulfur xenobiotic acceptors; endogenous substrate unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73B5 is a consistently safener- and stress-inducible D-group UGT. 2. It has low but measurable xenobiotic conjugation activity. 3. Evidence cited in the paper links it to defence-related protection in Arabidopsis.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering water-stress or detoxification responses, but likely unsuitable for direct xenobiotic detoxification enhancement alone because no activity was detected on the main substrate panel.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Known glucosyltransferase induced by stress; in this paper it showed no activity toward the main xenobiotic panel tested, though cited literature indicates activity on TNT catabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major safener- and stress-inducible group L UGT in Arabidopsis; likely acts on endogenous stress metabolites rather than the tested xenobiotics.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray induction after fenclorim treatment; Genevestigator signature analysis; negative catalytic screen on 11 xenobiotic substrates; cited prior functional evidence.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Likely acts on alternative endogenous or reactive-catabolite substrates rather than the panel end products
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fenclorim treatment; peroxide exposure and water-stress links cited from prior work.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Most highly and widely up-regulated Arabidopsis group L UGT in safener/stress signatures.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes that UGT74E2 had no activity toward the main xenobiotic panel, implying its biological role is more likely in endogenous stress metabolite regulation; cited as a modulator of water stress tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knock-down was cited to enhance resistance to P. syringae, indicating possible defence tradeoffs.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Unresolved endogenous stress-metabolite glucosides; TNT-catabolite conjugates cited from previous work
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Unresolved endogenous stress metabolites; not the major xenobiotics tested here
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Major stress-inducible node, not a proven catalytic bottleneck for the tested substrates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT74E2 is one of the strongest safener- and stress-induced UGTs in Arabidopsis. 2. It did not glycosylate the main xenobiotic panel used here. 3. The paper interprets it as a likely regulator of endogenous stress-metabolite homeostasis rather than general xenobiotic detoxification.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate marker or engineering target for cereal safener responsiveness; may be useful in herbicide-protection breeding if coupled to high-activity detox enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putative UDP-glycosyltransferase; exact substrate reaction was not biochemically defined in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Identified as one of the rice D-group UGTs most commonly up-regulated across fenclorim-like conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Rice microarray transcript profiling after fenclorim treatment and Genevestigator signature comparison.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Likely contributes to formation of stress/detoxification glucosides, but direct product not established
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fenclorim 100 µM for 4 h/24 h in rice N1 suspension cultures.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly recurrent induction across top rice conditions similar to fenclorim treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transcriptomic evidence only in this paper; no direct catalytic assay reported.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Unknown; stress-detoxification rewiring may affect endogenous metabolite balance.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT98B1 was one of the rice UGTs most consistently associated with fenclorim-like signatures. 2. It belongs to the rice D-group stress/safener-responsive module. 3. Its direct substrate specificity was not resolved in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential cereal safener-response marker/target, but direct catalytic function must be validated before deployment.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putative UDP-glycosyltransferase reaction not directly biochemically defined in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: One of the rice UGTs most broadly associated with fenclorim-like induction signatures.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Rice fenclorim microarray and Genevestigator meta-analysis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Presumed contribution to detoxification glucosides, not directly shown
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fenclorim 100 µM for 4 h/24 h in N1 rice suspension cultures.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced across multiple rice conditions similar to fenclorim treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transcript-level evidence only in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT701A1 is part of the rice fenclorim-responsive UGT set. 2. It was highlighted among recurrently induced rice UGTs. 3. Direct enzymatic substrate specificity was not determined here.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate rice safener-response marker; requires functional validation before engineering use.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putative UDP-glycosyltransferase reaction not defined biochemically in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Recurrently induced rice D-group UGT in fenclorim-like expression signatures.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Rice microarray profiling and Genevestigator signature analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Frequently induced across rice conditions similar to fenclorim treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT703A2 is a rice safener-responsive D-group UGT. 2. It appeared among the most recurrently induced rice UGTs. 3. Its biochemical substrate spectrum was not resolved here.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate rice safener-response gene for cereal improvement pipelines; direct metabolic function requires follow-up.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putative UDP-glycosyltransferase reaction not directly characterized here.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of the recurrently induced rice D-group UGT set associated with fenclorim response.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Rice microarray transcript profiling and Genevestigator comparison.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Frequently induced in rice conditions most similar to fenclorim treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT703A4 belongs to the rice D-group safener-responsive UGT module. 2. It was highlighted among recurrently induced genes in rice. 3. The study did not biochemically resolve its substrates.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential rice orthologue marker of the Arabidopsis UGT74E2-like stress module; may be useful for stress-coupled detoxification breeding.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putative UDP-glycosyltransferase reaction not biochemically defined in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Rice group L UGT recurrently induced in fenclorim-like conditions; orthologous to strongly induced Arabidopsis UGT74E2.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Rice microarray transcript profiling and comparative phylogeny/Genevestigator analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced across multiple rice conditions similar to fenclorim exposure.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as an orthologue of Arabidopsis UGT74E2 in the rice stress/safener response.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Rice group L safener/stress-inducible UGT module
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Unknown; may impact endogenous stress signaling metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT74H3 is a rice L-group UGT associated with fenclorim-like induction. 2. It is considered orthologous to Arabidopsis UGT74E2. 3. The study supports a conserved L-group stress-response module across species.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Safener-inducible cytokinin-related O-glucosylation / detoxification-associated UGT response
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Interesting candidate if coupling safener response with cytokinin metabolism is desired; however direct contribution to herbicide detoxification was not shown.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putative zeatin/cytokinin O-glucosyltransferase-like reaction inferred from orthology to maize cis-zeatin-O-glucosyltransferase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Uniquely induced rice group O enzyme after fenclorim treatment, unlike Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Rice microarray expression profiling and phylogenetic assignment.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Likely forms cytokinin O-glucosides rather than the xenobiotic glucosides assayed in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fenclorim 100 µM treatment in rice suspension cultures.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fenclorim inducible in rice; unique among rice group O UGTs in this response.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assigned in this paper to subfamily UGT93B; discussed as orthologue of maize cis-zeatin-O-glucosyltransferase UGT93B1.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could perturb cytokinin homeostasis if manipulated.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Rice UGT93B was uniquely induced by fenclorim among group O enzymes. 2. Phylogeny linked it to maize cis-zeatin-O-glucosyltransferase. 3. This indicates species-specific safener recruitment of a cytokinin-related UGT branch in rice.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hormone homeostasis and possibly safener-linked stress adjustment

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Safener-inducible cytokinin-related glycosylation / detoxification-associated UGT response
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible target linking safener response and cytokinin metabolism, but direct metabolic contribution to herbicide protection remains unresolved.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putative UDP-glycosyltransferase likely linked to cytokinin metabolism, based on discussion only.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Fenclorim enhanced expression of rice group A UGT91C1, suggesting possible links to cytokinin metabolism in safening.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Rice microarray induction analysis after fenclorim treatment.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Likely forms hormone glycosides; direct product not shown
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fenclorim 100 µM in rice N1 suspension cultures.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes potential links of UGT91C1 to cytokinin metabolism but does not directly assay activity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential changes in cytokinin homeostasis and growth if manipulated.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT91C1 was induced by fenclorim in rice. 2. The paper highlights it as potentially linked to cytokinin metabolism. 3. Its direct substrate specificity was not determined in this study.
